title: calyptrogyne elata
intro: Calyptrogyne elata, also known as the Guianese palm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Calyptrogyne elata include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
